pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Spring Boot parent from 2.7.4 to 3.3.1 to align with Java 17 and Spring Boot 3 ecosystem.
Set the target Java version property to 17 to compile against the new JDK.
Upgrade JWT library version to 4.4.0 for compatibility with newer dependencies.
Add lombok-mapstruct-binding dependency to resolve annotation processor conflicts between Lombok and MapStruct.
Upgrade OpenAPI/Swagger documentation library to version 2.6.0 compatible with Spring Boot 3.
Remove the explicit Apache Commons Lang3 dependency, likely relying on transitive dependencies or removing unused code.
Switch from springdoc-openapi-ui to springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui to support Spring Boot 3.
Remove the exclusion of junit-vintage-engine from spring-boot-starter-test, as it is no longer included by default in Spring Boot 3.
Update groupId, artifactId, and version to reflect the new project identity and versioning.
Update project description and developer name to reflect current state and correct spelling.

README.md.general_idea: Update the README to reflect the new Spring Boot version (3.3.1) corresponding to the Java 17 migration.
Clarify the Swagger dependency description to Open API.
Add a section detailing how to customize configuration files like application.yml and SecurityConfiguration.java.
Add instructions for starting the database using Docker Compose before running the application.
README.md.human_impl:
First you need to make sure that the database is up. 
If you're using Docker, you can use ```docker compose up -d``` command.

README.md.general_idea: Add a link to the Postman collection for API testing.
README.md.human_impl:
## Postman Collection

- [You can access the Postman collection here...]

README.md.general_idea: Fix the markdown reference link syntax for the Angular boilerplate link at the bottom of the file.

docker-compose.yml.general_idea: Removed the deprecated 'version' field from docker-compose.yml to align with modern Compose specification standards.
Renamed the database container from 'db_postgres' to 'db' for consistency with service names.
Added a health check to the PostgreSQL service to ensure the database is ready before dependent services start.
Added the application service definition to docker-compose.yml to orchestrate the Spring Boot container alongside the database.
Configured environment variables for the application service to connect to the database.
Added a health check to the application service using the Spring Boot Actuator endpoint.
docker-compose.yml.human_impl:
healthcheck:
      test: "curl --fail --silent localhost:8080/actuator/health/readiness | grep UP || exit 1"

docker-compose.yml.general_idea: Defined a dependency condition so the application waits for the database to be healthy before starting.

src/main/resources/application.yml.general_idea: Enable HTTP/2 support for the server, a feature often enabled by default or recommended in newer Spring Boot versions.
Refactor database connection properties to use environment variables with defaults for better security and deployment flexibility.
Explicitly name the HikariCP connection pool for better observability in logs and monitoring.
Remove the explicit PostgreSQL dialect configuration as it is auto-detected in newer Hibernate/Spring Boot versions.
Disable the Open Session In View pattern to prevent lazy loading issues and improve performance, a best practice in modern Spring Boot.
Explicitly disable JTA transaction management as it is not required for this application setup.
Remove deprecated or unnecessary SpringDoc configuration properties that are no longer needed or supported in the newer version.
Enable Kubernetes-style health probes (liveness/readiness) in the management endpoints.
Update the logging level configuration to match the new package structure after the artifact ID and package name change.
Update Swagger/OpenAPI metadata to reflect the new Spring Boot version and project details.
